title: Doctrine + skill prose: two-kind handoff rule (with transport), multi-session etiquette, census anchors (RED-first)
action: Align the always-loaded doctrine and skill prose with the shipped fresh-session flow — PROJECTION ONLY: restate what docs/specs/workflow-state.md B12-B16 already record as shipped, never new behavior. (1) AGENTS.block.md template (and the rendered block in root AGENTS.md — keep both in step; the census suite compares them): replace the blanket never-auto-resume HANDOFF rule with the two-kind rule, CARRYING ITS TRANSPORT per doctrine-layer B3a / critical rule 13 precedent: pause (and any record without a kind) = present and WAIT, never auto-resume — verbatim strength preserved; planned-next = written only via `bee state handoff write` after a capped, verify-green cell with the next cell claimed (`bee cells claim-next`), adopted automatically only at a fresh session (clear/startup); add one short multi-session etiquette rule: sessions coordinate through lanes/claims/holds — a hold deny names the holder and expiry, pick other work, never work around the guard. (2) skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md Session Scout HANDOFF bullet + references/routing-and-contracts.md HANDOFF mentions: kind-aware (pause = wait unchanged; planned-next presents its start-now instruction). (3) skills/bee-executing/SKILL.md + skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md: one short section each — when a task finishes verified and approved work remains, the finish→claim-next→planned-next-handoff→ask-the-user-to-/clear flow is available; the agent offers it, the user decides (never auto-/clear). (4) Census anchor rows in test_lib.mjs (B4/R2 pattern, RED-first): distinctive phrases of the new rule asserted on BOTH AGENTS.block.md template and root AGENTS.md; keep every existing census row green. NOTE: state.mjs is in files ONLY in case the AGENTS block render needs its BEE_VERSION touchpoint — do not change behavior; .bee/bin vendored siblings via cmp if any template lib file is touched. Existing rows extended, never modified.

truths: The standing sheet states the two-kind rule with its transport (the exact verbs), and census rows pin its anchors on both doctrine surfaces (template + rendered AGENTS.md)
Root AGENTS.md's block is hand-edited IN THIS CELL to stay byte-identical with the template (fsh-13's render only normalizes it later; deferring it ships a red census)
The pause-kind wait rule keeps its verbatim strength; kindless records are named pause explicitly
Executing/swarming prose describes the offer-the-flow behavior without mandating auto-/clear
Every pre-existing census and suite row passes unmodified

artifacts.substantive: two-kind handoff rule with transport + multi-session etiquette rule

key_links: census rows in test_lib.mjs grep the new anchors on both surfaces
prohibitions: No behavior/lib changes (prose + census rows only)
No weakening of the pause wait rule
No origination — nothing the specs do not already record as shipped
No version bump (fsh-13 owns release prep)
New prose avoids the banned retired-review phrases the census forbids

trace.outcome: Doctrine + skill prose now state the shipped two-kind handoff rule with its transport verbs (bee state handoff write --kind planned-next / bee cells claim-next / bee state handoff adopt) and a new multi-session etiquette rule (hold denies name the holder+expiry, pick other work, never work around the guard). Root AGENTS.md's rendered block was hand-edited to stay byte-identical with the template. bee-hive SKILL.md + routing-and-contracts.md HANDOFF mentions are now kind-aware; bee-executing/bee-swarming each carry a short offer-only fresh-session-handoff section. RED-first census check added to test_lib.mjs, confirmed failing against the pre-edit doctrine text via git stash, then green after the edits; all 4 pre-existing verify commands pass (292/121/141-rows/ALL PASS).
